title: Reading Nexova's Income Statement
context: Your first week at Nexova Ltd. The CFO hands you last year's income statement and asks you to demonstrate you can read it.

financial_data.title: Nexova Ltd — Income Statement FY2025 (€ thousands)

questions.text: What is Nexova's gross margin? (enter as a whole number, e.g. 70 for 70%)
Which is the largest operating expense category?
What is Nexova's net profit margin? (round to nearest whole number)

title: Analysing the Balance Sheet
context: The CFO wants to know if you can assess Nexova's financial health from the balance sheet.

financial_data.title: Nexova Ltd — Balance Sheet FY2025 (€ thousands)

questions.text: What is Nexova's current ratio? (round to 1 decimal place, e.g. 2.1)
What is Nexova's working capital? (in € thousands)
What does a current ratio above 2.0 indicate?
options: The company can comfortably cover short-term obligations
The company has too much inventory
questions.correct_answer: The company can comfortably cover short-term obligations

title: Understanding the Cash Flow Statement
context: Net income is not the same as cash. The CFO tests whether you understand the difference.

financial_data.title: Nexova Ltd — Cash Flow Statement FY2025 (€ thousands)

questions.text: What is Nexova's free cash flow? (Operating CF minus CapEx, in € thousands)
Why is net cash from operations (€850k) higher than net income (€720k)?
options: Non-cash charges like depreciation are added back and working capital improved
questions.correct_answer: Non-cash charges like depreciation are added back and working capital improved

questions.text: Purchasing equipment (€120k) appears under which section of the cash flow statement?
